tool.name: FastQC

exit_code.description: FastQC returned non zero exit code
FastQC returned non zero exit code

regex.match: There is insufficient memory for the Java Runtime Environment

tool.command:
#import re #set input_name = re.sub('[^\w\-\s]', '_', str($input_file.element_identifier))
#if $input_file.ext.endswith('.gz'): #set input_file_sl = $input_name + '.gz' #elif $input_file.ext.endswith('.bz2'): #set input_file_sl = $input_name + '.bz2' #else #set input_file_sl = $input_name #end if
#if 'bam' in $input_file.ext: #set format = 'bam' #elif 'sam' in $input_file.ext: #set format = 'sam' #else #set format = 'fastq' #end if
ln -s '${input_file}' '${input_file_sl}' && mkdir -p '${html_file.files_path}' && fastqc --outdir '${html_file.files_path}' #if $contaminants.dataset and str($contaminants) > '' --contaminants '${contaminants}' #end if
#if $adapters.dataset and str($adapters) > '' --adapters '${adapters}' #end if
#if $limits.dataset and str($limits) > '' --limits '${limits}' #end if --threads \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-2} --dir \${TEMP:-\$_GALAXY_JOB_TMP_DIR} --quiet --extract #if $min_length: --min_length $min_length #end if $nogroup --kmers $kmers -f '${format}' '${input_file_sl}'
&& cp '${html_file.files_path}'/*/fastqc_data.txt output.txt && cp '${html_file.files_path}'/*\.html output.html

param.name: input_file
param.label: Raw read data from your current history

param.name: contaminants
param.label: Contaminant list
param.help: tab delimited file with 2 columns: name and sequence. For example: Illumina Small RNA RT Primer CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGA

param.label: Adapter list
param.help: List of adapters adapter sequences which will be explicity searched against the library. It should be a tab-delimited file with 2 columns: name and sequence.

param.name: limits
param.label: Submodule and Limit specifing file
param.help: a file that specifies which submodules are to be executed (default=all) and also specifies the thresholds for the each submodules warning parameter

param.label: Disable grouping of bases for reads >50bp
param.help: Using this option will cause fastqc to crash and burn if you use it on really long reads, and your plots may end up a ridiculous size. You have been warned!

param.label: Lower limit on the length of the sequence to be shown in the report
param.help: As long as you set this to a value greater or equal to your longest read length then this will be the sequence length used to create your read groups. This can be useful for making directly comaparable statistics from datasets with somewhat variable read lengths.

param.label: Length of Kmer to look for
param.help: Note: the Kmer test is disabled and needs to be enabled using a custom Submodule and limits file

tool.help:
**Purpose**
FastQC aims to provide a simple way to do some quality control checks on raw sequence data coming from high throughput sequencing pipelines.
See: http://www.bioinformatics.babraham.ac.uk/projects/fastqc/
